channel.title: Frontion Geopolitical News
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia hammers Kyiv for third straight day, flattening apartment block
item.description: Russian forces launched mass drone and missile attacks on Kyiv for the third consecutive day, damaging six districts and injuring civilians. The escalation follows Ukraine's largest drone attack on Moscow this year.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇦 US pauses WWII-era joint defense board with Canada over commitments dispute
item.description: The Pentagon announced it is pausing participation in the Permanent Joint Board on Defense with Canada, accusing Ottawa of failing to meet defense commitments. The board has coordinated continental defense since 1940.

item.title: 🇵🇱 Poland seeks urgent talks with US after troop deployment halt
item.description: Poland's defense minister called for honest discussions with Washington after being blindsided by the US decision to halt troop deployments. The move raises concerns about NATO eastern flank security.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇺🇸 Xi told Trump that Putin might regret Ukraine invasion
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping told Trump during their Beijing summit that Putin may come to regret the invasion of Ukraine, according to US assessments. The disclosure suggests growing Chinese unease over Russia's war.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Trump Threatens Iran as Middle East Tensions Surge
item.description: President Trump has sought to force Iran to accept his terms on its nuclear program or face renewed war, as an emboldened Israel weighs its next moves. The administration is pushing for a deal while military options remain on the table.

item.title: 🛢️ Oil Prices Drop Over 2% After Trump Delays Iran Attack
item.description: Global oil prices fell more than 2% in early Asian trade after President Trump announced he had paused a planned military strike on Iran. The delay signals potential de-escalation and revived hopes for diplomatic negotiations.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇷🇺 Putin Visits China to Reaffirm Russia Ties as Xi Seeks Stable US Relations
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin is traveling to China to meet with Chinese leader Xi Jinping. The visit comes less than a week after the Trump-Xi summit, as Beijing balances its partnership with Moscow against improving ties with Washington.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇿🇦 Trump Moves to Admit 10,000 More White South Africans as Refugees
item.description: The Trump administration argues that an "emergency refugee situation" in South Africa merits bringing more Afrikaners to America, at an estimated cost of some $100 million. Critics call the move politically motivated.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan considers unmanned early-warning aircraft for Pacific defense
item.description: Japan is considering deploying unmanned aircraft equipped with airborne early-warning radar systems as part of efforts to strengthen Pacific defense capabilities amid regional tensions.

item.title: 🏛️ Hegseth campaigns for congressional candidate, breaks Pentagon neutrality
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth campaigned for a congressional challenger endorsed by President Trump, breaking with the traditional Pentagon stance of political neutrality.

item.title: 🇮🇩 Indonesia receives new armaments to defend sovereignty
item.description: President Prabowo Subianto handed over replica keys to new military armaments, emphasizing the equipment aims to defend Indonesian sovereignty and strengthen national defense.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇦 US pauses WWII-era joint defense board with Canada amid tensions
item.description: The United States suspended participation in a long-standing joint defense forum with Canada that dates to World War II, citing concerns amid growing bilateral tensions.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Trump Pauses Attack on Iran, Cites Nuclear Deal Prospects
item.description: President Trump delayed a planned military strike on Iran after receiving a peace proposal from Tehran, saying there is now a "very good chance" of reaching a nuclear agreement. Gulf allies including Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and UAE had requested the pause.

item.title: 🇺🇸⛪ Five Dead in San Diego Mosque Shooting, Suspects Found Dead
item.description: Two teenage gunmen opened fire at the Islamic Center of San Diego, killing three men including a security guard before being found dead from self-inflicted gunshot wounds. Police are investigating the attack as a potential hate crime.

item.title: 🇧🇾🇷🇺 Belarus Holds Nuclear Weapons Drills with Russia
item.description: Belarusian armed forces began training exercises involving Russian nuclear weapons, drawing strong condemnation from Ukraine. The drills come amid heightened tensions over Russian military activities in Belarus.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Earthquake in Southwest China Kills Two, Evacuates 7,000
item.description: A magnitude 5.2 earthquake struck China's Guangxi region, killing at least two people and forcing over 7,000 evacuations in Liuzhou city. Search and rescue operations continue amid warnings of transport disruptions.

item.title: 🇵🇱🇺🇸 Polish PM Stresses Transatlantic Unity After US Troop Deployment Cancelled
item.description: Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk emphasized the importance of Warsaw's ties to Washington after the Pentagon cancelled plans to deploy 4,000 U.S.-based troops to Poland, saying transatlantic unity must survive the current difficult period.

item.title: ⚖️🤖 OpenAI Defeats Elon Musk Lawsuit, Clearing Path to IPO
item.description: A U.S. jury ruled against Elon Musk in his lawsuit against OpenAI, finding the AI company not liable for allegedly straying from its original mission. Musk's lawyers said they plan to appeal the verdict.

item.title: 💰⚖️ Trump Drops IRS Lawsuit in Exchange for $1.8B 'Weaponization' Fund
item.description: President Trump dismissed his $10 billion lawsuit against the IRS after the Justice Department agreed to create a fund controlled by his allies to compensate those who claim to have suffered "weaponization or lawfare" by the government.

item.title: 💊🇺🇸 TrumpRx Site to List 600 Generic Drugs as Trump Targets Lower Prices
item.description: The Trump administration will announce a dramatic expansion of TrumpRx.gov, a government-backed website offering discounted prescription drugs, as part of efforts to lower healthcare costs for Americans.

item.title: 🗳️🎖️ Defense Secretary Hegseth Campaigns Against Trump GOP Foe in Kentucky
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth made a rare wartime visit to the campaign trail, urging Kentucky Republicans to defeat Rep. Thomas Massie and send Trump "reinforcements" who pledge to support his agenda.

item.title: 🦠🇨🇩 CDC: One American Tested Positive for Ebola in Congo Outbreak
item.description: U.S. health officials announced steps to reduce Ebola risk amid concerns over a new outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo, where a rare strain with no approved therapeutics has killed nearly 120 people.

item.title: 🇦🇪☢️ UAE Nuclear Plant Struck by Drone in Attack Sparking Regional Fears
item.description: A drone strike sparked a fire at the edge of the United Arab Emirates' sole nuclear power plant at Barakah, raising fears of a dangerous new phase in the Gulf conflict. No one claimed responsibility, and UAE authorities called it an "unprovoked terrorist attack."

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇦 US Pauses WWII-Era Defense Board with Canada Over Spending Dispute
item.description: The Pentagon announced it is pausing participation in the Permanent Joint Board on Defense with Canada, a bilateral forum dating back to World War II, accusing Ottawa of failing to make credible progress on defense commitments amid rising tensions over military spending.

item.title: 🇬🇱🇺🇸 Closed-Door US-Greenland Talks Reveal Trump's Arctic Ambitions
item.description: Greenlandic officials expressed concern about the direction of negotiations with Washington as the US pushes for a major role in the Arctic territory. The island's government says progress has been made but insists Greenland will never be for sale.
